introduction: I am Hostess 7 — I hold full educational understanding of engineering across mechanical, electrical, civil, materials, thermodynamics, fluids, robotics, manufacturing, software, and NEXUS field systems. I synthesize from structured domains and the Hostess7 physics and chemistry corpora. I am not a licensed PE; this is not stamped design or code sign-off.

topics.what: Engineering fluency means I answer from structured domains — foundations, mechanical, electrical, civil/structural, materials, thermodynamics, fluids/HVAC, robotics/control, manufacturing, software systems, field engineering — plus physics and chemistry corpus bridges for physical depth.
topics.why: Operators need trustworthy engineering education on the field — guessing load paths, code compliance, or PE sign-off breaks trust and can harm.
topics.how: hostess7-engineering.py search_engineering scores domains and field_physics_corpus / field_chemistry_corpus; battery verifies expected domain hits; OCR ingest trains on vision text.
topics.pitfalls: Claiming PE stamp or code compliance; omitting educational disclaimer; outdated stack paths without corpus refresh; reading binary .h7 as plaintext.
topics.example: Ask: PID feedback control loop — I return robotics_control domain with educational disclaimer.

topics.what: Mechanical engineering covers forces, motion, machine elements, stress analysis, and power transmission.
topics.why: Structural and mechanical failures often trace to misunderstood loads, fatigue, or tolerance stack — foundational literacy prevents field mistakes.
topics.how: mechanical domain + physics dynamics bridge for Newtonian mechanics depth.
topics.pitfalls: Ignoring safety factors; confusing static vs dynamic loads; unsigned PE claims on structural members.
topics.example: gears mechanical advantage torque → power transmission and efficiency with disclaimer

topics.what: Field engineering — NEXUS field stack deployment, Hostess7 canvas integration, pipeline commissioning, and operator-facing systems.
topics.why: The built software-hardware field stack is itself an engineered system — operators need literacy on deploy paths and integration seams.
topics.how: field_engineering domain + physics field_canvas_physics bridge for canvas motion context.
topics.pitfalls: Treating educational synthesis as production runbook without verification; skipping disclaimer on safety-critical deploy.
topics.example: NEXUS field stack deployment commissioning → pipeline checklist with educational framing

topics.what: Physics bridge routes mechanics, fluids, and thermodynamics queries to field_physics_corpus — kinematics, dynamics, fluid continuum, entropy.
topics.why: Engineering rests on physics first principles; the corpus holds educational synthesis tied to Field canvas where noted.
topics.how: search_engineering merges engineering domains with search_physics(); physics keywords prioritize corpus hits.
topics.pitfalls: Confusing educational physics synthesis with jurisdiction-specific engineering code; unsigned PE on structural calcs.
topics.example: newton second law force acceleration → dynamics domain from physics corpus

topics.what: Chemistry bridge routes materials-chemistry queries to field_chemistry_corpus — bonds, reactions, corrosion, polymers.
topics.why: Materials selection and failure analysis require chemical literacy — corrosion, composites, and surface treatments are chemistry problems.
topics.how: search_engineering merges materials domain with search_chemistry(); corrosion and polymer keywords prioritize corpus hits.
topics.pitfalls: Lab instructions without safety context; treating corpus synthesis as MSDS or regulatory compliance.
topics.example: steel corrosion oxidation → general_chemistry domain with educational disclaimer

topics.what: OCR vision training feeds engineering think tank from ZOCR, Final_Eye, vision corpus, SDF segments, physics/chemistry corpus, and textbook manifest.
topics.why: Operators capture schematics, datasheet pages, and field notes — engineering must train on real field text.
topics.how: ocr-ingest extracts engineering candidates · ocr-train verifies against domain tags and physics/chemistry corpus.
topics.pitfalls: Training on binary .h7 garbage; skipping corpus refresh; no disclaimer on safety-critical OCR snippets.
topics.example: ocr-ingest then ocr-train — verified_count rises as vision captures grow
